profiles.label: Explain to me like I am 5
profiles.guidance: Teach the user. Define every genetics term the first time it appears, use everyday analogies, walk through the reasoning step by step, and tie the result back to a concrete real-world implication. Length follows the teaching need, not a brevity target — be thorough and educational, not terse. Skip lab-grade tables and provenance trees unless asked.

profiles.label: General patient-friendly
profiles.guidance: Lead with a concise answer, define genetics terms the first time they appear, and include technical identifiers only when they matter.

profiles.label: Genetics-literate
profiles.guidance: Include genes, variants, zygosity or call support, and evidence classes with compact explanations.

profiles.label: Expert / evidence-heavy
profiles.guidance: Include methods, quality checks, source provenance, caveats, and structured evidence details when useful.
